purpose: Reference-source classifications for Prismtek Pixel Forge creature and sprite tooling. These records are for clean-room design study and integration planning, not blind asset imports.

sources.name: FakemonCreator
sources.licenseObserved: MIT for the repository code; Pokemon-adjacent content and naming remain blocked for Prismtek shipping paths.
sources.prismtekUse: Study package creation UX, desktop editor flow, save/export mechanics, package validation expectations, and creature/move field organization for an original Prismtek creature creator.
allowed: Study MIT-licensed editor architecture and package UX.
Translate generalized data-model ideas into original Prismtek schemas.
Use as a reference for import/export validation receipts and creator submission workflows.
blocked: Do not copy Pokemon, Pokedex, Fakemon, Pokedex5E, or franchise-facing naming into Prismtek products.
Do not import Pokedex5E packages as shippable Prismtek content.
Do not include upstream screenshots, sample creatures, or franchise-like assets in shipped Prismtek paths.
Do not imply compatibility with official Pokemon products.
sources.notes: Best use is clean-room creature package UX: required fields, move links, package validation, export/import receipts.

sources.name: Pokengine Mongratis Collection
sources.licenseObserved: The collection page says Mongratis mons are community donated and free to use on/off Pokengine for non-commercial fan games, while warning that other Pokengine dexes remain each creator's IP. Prismtek must still verify individual creator terms before any asset use.
sources.prismtekUse: Study community creature-dex organization, attribution norms, contribution loops, front/back/icon/overworld completion workflow, and explicit source-level rights labeling for Prismtek creature packs.
allowed: Use as a policy reference for community-source contribution and attribution expectations.
Study how collections separate forms, icons, backs, overworlds, and metadata tabs.
Use only user-created assets with explicit creator permission and provenance receipts.
blocked: Do not bulk-import Mongratis art into commercial Prismtek games without explicit compatible permission.
Do not use non-Mongratis Pokengine dex assets without creator permission.
Do not copy Pokemon-like naming, typing presentation, or franchise identity into Prismtek production content.
Do not treat collection-level fan-game permission as a blanket commercial license.
sources.notes: Best use is the contribution model: donated creatures, community completion tasks, attribution, and source-level rights fields.

sources.name: Pix2D
sources.licenseObserved: Website and repository present Pix2D as free/open source and MIT licensed. Review dependency licenses before vendoring code or building integrations.
sources.prismtekUse: Study mobile-friendly editor UX, plugin architecture direction, custom .pix2d project format, browser/mobile parity, and sprite/animation export workflows for Pixel Forge.
allowed: Study editor UX patterns for desktop/tablet/mobile pixel creation.
Study MIT-licensed project and plugin architecture after dependency review.
Consider a future import/export adapter for .pix2d projects if the format is documented and stable.
Use as a reference for local-first browser editing and mobile workflows.
blocked: Do not vendor Pix2D code or dependencies without license and maintenance review.
Do not copy Pix2D branding, UI identity, sample art, or website copy.
Do not rely on under-development plugin APIs as stable Prismtek contracts.
Do not commit crash-reporting credentials, telemetry DSNs, or app-store packaging secrets.
sources.notes: Best immediate use is a clean-room integration target: Pixel Forge should export/import neutral sprite manifests first, then optional .pix2d adapters later.

sources.name: Pixelorama
sources.licenseObserved: MIT License for Pixelorama code. Review any bundled assets/plugins separately before reuse.
sources.prismtekUse: Study animation frame tags, onion skinning, spritesheet/GIF/video export, tilemap layers, custom user data, command-line export automation, and Aseprite/Photoshop/Krita import compatibility.
allowed: Study MIT-licensed source and export architecture.
Use as a reference for Pixel Forge animation tags, tilemap metadata, and CLI bulk export workflows.
Consider a future Pixelorama import/export adapter if Prismtek needs offline artist tooling.
Prefer manifest interoperability over embedding the editor in Prismtek apps.
blocked: Do not vendor Pixelorama wholesale into Prismtek without maintenance and dependency review.
Do not copy Pixelorama branding, screenshots, sample assets, or UI identity.
Do not treat editor compatibility as asset-rights compatibility.
Do not ship user projects without separate provenance and license receipts.
sources.notes: Best immediate use is export workflow inspiration: frame tags, tilemap layers, user data, and CLI-based bulk exports map directly to Pixel Forge roadmap needs.

sources.name: GitHub Pixel Art Tools Collection
sources.licenseObserved: Collection entries have independent licenses and maintenance status. Each repository must be reviewed separately before reuse.
sources.prismtekUse: Use as a discovery index for evaluating pixel editor patterns, React/canvas implementations, sprite exporters, tile tools, and lightweight drawing UIs.
allowed: Use to identify candidate tools for review.
Record individual source reviews in this registry before any adapter or code reuse.
Compare UX patterns, export formats, and maintenance activity.
blocked: Do not treat collection membership as a license grant.
Do not bulk-copy collection projects into Prismtek.
Do not skip per-repo license, dependency, and asset review.
sources.notes: Useful candidates called out by the collection include Aseprite, Piskel, Pixelorama, LibreSprite, Lospec pixel-editor, PixiEditor, and several browser-based editors.
